description_long: This bollard is a tall, rectangular stone post with a weathered, rough surface and a pointed, beveled top. The stone appears aged, with visible discoloration, moss, and faint engravings or inscriptions on its front and side faces. The base is set on a square platform of stone tiles, suggesting it is intended for permanent installation. The rear face is plain, with a rectangular patch that may indicate a previous attachment or repair. The overall form is monolithic and solid, with no visible moving parts or connectors. Its substantial mass and robust construction make it suitable for use as a boundary marker or access control barrier in urban environments.
description: A tall, weathered stone bollard with a pointed top, faint engravings, and a solid square base set on stone tiles.
description_view_0: Front view shows faint engravings and the pointed top, with visible weathering and discoloration.
description_view_1: Side view reveals more engravings and a smoother surface, with less discoloration than the front.
description_view_2: Rear view is plain, featuring a rectangular patch and no visible engravings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view is darker and more heavily weathered, with minimal visible markings.
